feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: To the Zoomer, the top two seem as quaint as "werewolf" and "vampire" seem to th…
entry.content: To the Zoomer, the top two seem as quaint as "werewolf" and "vampire" seem to the millennial. Horror seems silly if you don't know the real fear it's representing. The problems Zoomers face are simulacrum and alienation.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Lazenby Bond OHMSS
entry.content: Should George Lazenby have been James Bond during the OHMSS film, or no? Could Sean Connery have made that movie any better than it was with Lazenby as 007?

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >Does anyone believe in mythology.. vampires, ghost, mermaids the things ur not …
entry.content: >Does anyone believe in mythology.. vampires, ghost, mermaids the things ur not supposed to talk about... why are those the exact topics that serve real interest.. other worlds, the ocean is the most mysterious.. watching too much of the discovery channel of course, very few people can go to the deepest parts so much has not been seen. there's so much we still don't know beyond treasure... I feel like perhaps there might be different worlds there!!!! I'm a dreamer... I've read millions of books

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >Walsh recalled giving Don Henley an accidental attempted circumcision during a …
entry.content: >Walsh recalled giving Don Henley an accidental attempted circumcision during a party at the latter's Los Angeles home on November 16, 1980. "Me, Don, Mick Taylor (the Foreigner guitarist), and Steve Miller were there, and Don was passed out naked on the living room floor. So I see his big ol' hog there looking like a pig wrapped in a sweater, and I dunno what I was thinking. I wasn't in my right mind back then, and I decided for some reason that it needed a trimming. So I get out my pocket knif

entry.title: /b/ Random: What does it mean to have a relationship with Jesus Christ
entry.content: It means you recognize your weakness in the eyes of God. Only through Jesus sacrifice of a perfect life and your acceptance of him as your personal savior may you gain Gods forgives of your own sins and all of our personal sins. Alway consider how God might view you more favorable (I use to think less detestable, but we are made in his image, its or actions only that render us detestable in his eyes). Accept that all things shall be in accordance with Gods will. AND, yes I am a sinner.

Someday, I'll accomplish my dream, I'll live in a world where there's only white people with Northern European traits. I'll be white just like them.

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Removing Telemetry from a Nissan
entry.content: Would have posted in a general but there was no Nissan gen here. I have a chance to get a great deal on a 2024 Nissan Versa S, but I am committed in trying to keep my privacy, so I want to disconnect anything that would spread telemetry of my car's usage data to the manufacturer. I read many Nissans have their TCUs behind the glove compartment but I have yet to see if this still holds true for the model I'm looking at. The bigger matter is that I've read DCing the TCU in a modern Nissan will als

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >Twenty years ago, Radiohead released “OK Computer,” a landmark album that was p…
entry.content: >Twenty years ago, Radiohead released “OK Computer,” a landmark album that was profoundly prog: grand and dystopian, with a lead single that was more than six minutes long. But when a reporter asked one of the members whether Radiohead had been influenced by Genesis and Pink Floyd, the answer was swift and categorical: “No. We all hate progressive rock music.”

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Is Aprilia literally blowing the doors off the motorcycle market with their tuareg 660?
entry.content: Pretty much every youtube review ends up being a video of how the reviewer decided to buy a tuareg for themselves after reviewing it. Thats how much ppl love this fucking thing. So easy and fun to ride if you are tall enough/have enough leg inseam. Its sort of a flex over short ppl to buy adv bikes like the tuareg bc if you are not at least 6 feet tall you cant ride them. Youre just gonna end up dropping them if you do and looking like an enormous faggot.

entry.content: >Destroys the internet by flooding it with low quality spam comments that can't be filtered >Destroys academia and makes degrees even more worthless with rampant cheating >Floods every image board / website with low quality images >Enables rampant cheating across every form of online competition imaginable >causes the prices of consumer electronics to skyrocket >makes any office job a living hell, now you constantly have to clean up after co worker's ai generated slop >doesn't even make money fo

entry.title: /g/ Technology: If you want to scan documents that contains images and text, you should use a 30…
entry.content: If you want to scan documents that contains images and text, you should use a 300 DPI (2480 x 3508 pixels) resolution and compress it as JPEG 90% (0.8 to 2 MB), and embed it into a PDF 1.4 or later. Thank me in the morning.

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /bln/ Blacked Lane #86
entry.content: Washington Edition Comfy weekend thread dedicated to bote queens and the anons who love them. Some 3D allowed (Koikatsu, Honey Select 2, etc) but no cosplayers. Don't talk about real-life stuff and keep extremes to yourself. No schizos and no drama. If you see a troll, hide post and keep going. previous thread:https://desuarchive.org/tras h/thread/84008163/#84008163

entry.title: /o/ Auto: i have a 2006 mercury grand marquis. i was sitting in the drive thru and the tem…
entry.content: i have a 2006 mercury grand marquis. i was sitting in the drive thru and the temp gauge was high. i shut off the car for a bit while waiting then when i got my food i turned off the A/C and drove home. while i was driving, the temp went back down to normal. i got to my driveway and parked, then turned the a/c on and the temp slowly went back up. idk if the AC changed anything. i also didn't see fluid in the coolant reservoir jiggling when i shook the car, though i'm not sure about that because i

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Secret Identities
entry.content: I never understood how for decades it was a written rule that vigilantes required secret identities to prevent reprisals against their loved ones. Yet, in the MCU, Tony Stark, Bruce Banner, Elliot Rodgers are all public figures. Yet no one thinks to kidnap Pepper or anything. Which then gets contradicted by Peter Parker's trilogy-wide plot revolving about his secret identity being leaked. How does this shit work?!

entry.content: whats the coast you ever tossed on a coin loss ?

entry.title: /int/ International: >Women in Switzerland gained the right to vote in federal elections after a refe…
entry.content: >Women in Switzerland gained the right to vote in federal elections after a referendum in February 1971. The first federal vote in which women were able to participate was the 31 October 1971 election of the Federal Assembly. However it was not until a 1990 decision by the Federal Supreme Court of Switzerland that women gained full voting rights in the final Swiss canton of Appenzell Innerrhoden. Why did they wait so long(compared with other Western countries) to grant women the right to vote?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Actual Hot Take on RPGs
entry.content: Any game that emulates pen'n'paper RPG mechanics is fucking shit and lazy. Seriously. ANY game with top down view and randomized RPG garbage sucks dick. The reason pen'n'paper RPGs exist and have mechanics like that is because you CAN NOT reliably cast a fireball or swing a sword or come up with a clever response in real life that your character would. But in a virtual, computer generated world YOU CAN. You can have your character cast an actual fireball and hit the enemies, you can swing your s

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: In days of yore I took multiple player characters through the first three hubs o…
entry.content: In days of yore I took multiple player characters through the first three hubs of VTMB, but I'd played it to completion only once. Last week, after more than a decade, I got the urge to dust off this janky old game and press all the way to the end with a Nosferatu (male). I had fun role-playing the quests as a festering sewer monster, but sadly, even with the latest patch and restoration content, the endgame was just as awful as I recalled. AGAIN I had to use godmode to get past Ming Xiao. My No

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: I have had some experiences which I can only explain as metaphysical, so I belie…
entry.content: I have had some experiences which I can only explain as metaphysical, so I believe in the afterlife. However, I am also I would say a logical person, I don't discredit material events for what they are. Because of this I am keenly aware many of my mind's aspect seem to be chemical and biological, given they can be influenced by even petty elements like the environment or how much water I drank. So it seems to be obvious to me my mind cannot exist as it is in the afterlife. There wouldn't be this

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: check out this nigger
/g/ Technology: Got a job as a field service technician while pretending to have a car. Currentl…
entry.content: Got a job as a field service technician while pretending to have a car. Currently I'm being trained so a colleague is driving me around while I watch he working. I don't even know how I'm supposed to walk around the city using public transport in rush hours with a bunch of boxes filled with computer parts. Do you think I'll be able to last at least a month to get some money? I'm already owing money to my mom. Anyone did something similar?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >everyone is going to abandon frontier models and agent harnesses so that they c…
entry.content: >everyone is going to abandon frontier models and agent harnesses so that they can save a few bucks on tokens Not sure what's with this Chinese propaganda. You don't see companies hosting on some chink cloud, or buying their employees Xiaomi phones. The West generally cares about reliability and SLAs and you don't get that with chinktech. The CCP has been running a surveillance state for decades now and despite having a camera on my every street corner, they never figured out LLMs, transformers,

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: System Shock
entry.content: Just finished system shock for the first time. Ace game, I highly recommend it. I didn't think much of it at first, but then got really hooked on it at research level. I think I'm going to replay it on the higher difficulty settings.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: How can this still happen in modern era?

What purpose do they serve other than providing the perfect environment for date rapists (the implications).
